seoTitle: Luc Rousseau | Product Manager / Product Owner resume
seoDescription: Luc Rousseau's resume as a Product Manager and Product Owner with a senior technical background: roadmap, prioritization, stakeholder alignment and delivery. Quebec and remote.
tagline: A product lead with a technical background: I turn business goals into a clear, prioritized, delivered roadmap.

summary.title: Profile
summary.content:
Product Manager and Product Owner with a senior technical background, I move the product from the "why" to "shipped". At Milesopedia I grew from senior full stack developer to Product Manager, then Product Lead: I can frame the value and understand what it costs to build.

I turn business goals into a clear, prioritized roadmap, align content, engineering and leadership, and hold the bar through delivery. My edge: realistic roadmaps and credibility with engineering teams, because I have been on the other side of the brief.

items.label: business goals into a clear, prioritized plan
content, dev and management aligned, shipped

items.title: Vision and roadmap
items.content: Business goals translated into a clear, sequenced, shared product plan.

items.title: Prioritization and trade-offs
items.content: Impact versus effort, mapped dependencies, owned decisions, including saying "not now".

items.title: Stakeholder alignment
items.content: Content, engineering and leadership around one direction and one shared language.

items.title: Delivery and measurable value
items.content: Release discipline and tracking of value actually shipped, not just features.

items.summary: Product partner for growing teams: roadmap, prioritization and delivery, with the credibility of a senior technical background.
bullets: Translate business goals and stakeholder expectations into a clear, prioritized, sequenced roadmap.
Arbitrate scope, priorities and dependencies, with an explicit frame for saying "not now".
Align product, content and development in Scrum/Kanban ceremonies; hold the quality bar through delivery.
A technical background that keeps roadmaps realistic and the relationship with engineering credible.

items.progression: Internal progression: senior full stack developer → Product Manager → Product Lead
items.summary: Multi-brand comparison network (credit cards, travel, personal finance), Canada and France.
bullets: Moved from development into product: cross-team coordination on a multi-brand headless program.
Strategic alignment across content, management and development; resolved friction points.
Steered and prioritized strategic initiatives; oversaw deliverables (deadlines, quality, measurable value).
Ensured every initiative delivered measurable value for users and internal stakeholders.
Team: 3 developers, 1 DevOps consultant, plus staff on parallel projects.
items.stack: Headless WordPress, React, PHP, MySQL, REST APIs

bullets: System architecture for loan analysis and comparison; phased planning (milestones, deliverables, timelines).
Coordination across wireframes, design, back-end and front-end through delivery.
UI prototypes and wireframes with designers; A/B testing and data analysis to steer decisions.
items.stack: React, Vue.js, Next.js, PHP, Laravel, GraphQL, REST, WordPress, Webpack, Docker, Tailwind

items.summary: Online mortgage lender, Canadian fintech ($302M Series E, $1.47B valuation).
bullets: Centralized system architecture, shared foundation for every initiative: consistency, efficiency, maintenance.
Adaptable grid to tailor each project while preserving a solid infrastructure.
Technical direction and mentoring of one developer; guidance aligned with architecture and best practices.
Recruiting and technical-skills assessments; monitoring, maintenance and continuous architecture optimization.
items.stack: React, Vue.js, Next.js, PHP, Laravel, GraphQL, REST, WordPress, Webpack, Docker, Tailwind, Git

items.summary: Custom web development studio: clients, delivery and team.
Custom websites and web applications for SMEs and agencies.

items.role: Art director, head of the interactive department
bullets: Creative direction and ownership of the interactive department.
